mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (below 1%) reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and lowers the opportunity cost of holding stocks versus cash, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or mean forward return during sub-1% FEDFUNDS periods is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, causing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days to turn negative.
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising real policy rate, proxied jointly by an increasing FEDFUNDS level combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth, tightens effective financial conditions beyond what nominal rates alone indicate, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY) shows Rank IC below 0.03 against 60d forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term does not outperform either single-series model in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing expansion regime, elevated housing starts (HOUST) signal robust consumer demand and construction-linked employment gains that feed through to broader corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon; this relationship weakens or reverses outside expansionary housing regimes.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between HOUST level/trend and 60d forward returns fails to exceed Rank IC of 0.03 during periods independently classified as housing expansions, or sign flips in more than 40% of expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving real economic output momentum that is quickly priced into equities, producing a positive short-horizon drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to the incremental supply-side signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and 5d forward returns stays below 0.03 across any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips in more than half of quarterly subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signals tightening labor market conditions that raise the probability of hawkish monetary policy responses, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both conditions co-occur, distinct from either series in isolation.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction signal shows Rank IC below 0.03 against 20d forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or fails to outperform a model using either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth month-over-month exceeds 0.5%, it signals a surge in consumer spending momentum that produces an immediate positive reaction in S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as analysts revise consumption-driven earnings estimates upward.
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 5d forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The directional impact of federal funds rate changes on equity returns flips depending on the monetary regime: during an easing cycle, falling FEDFUNDS supports positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days, while during a tightening cycle the same magnitude of change produces negative returns, because the marginal signal about growth versus inflation risk differs by regime.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle versus tightening cycle
failure_condition: The sign of the FEDFUNDS-return relationship does not differ significantly between independently classified easing and tightening regimes, or Rank IC within each regime subsample stays below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.
